title: Perumparampu Sri Mahadeva Temple
tagline: Perumparampu Sri Mahadeva Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Perumparampu Sri Mahadeva Temple is located at Edappal in Malappuram district.
overview.description: Perumparampu Sri Mahadeva Temple is located at Edappal in Malappuram district. The main deity of the temple is Shiva in the sanctum sanctorum facing west. It is believed that this temple is one of the 108 Shiva temples of Kerala and is installed by sage Parasurama dedicated to Shiva. The temple is located around 3km away from Edappal on the route of Edappal - Parappuram - Ayankalam Road.

topPlaces.name: Edappal
topPlaces.description: Edappal is a town which lies near to the town of Ponnani in Ponnani taluk, Malappuram district, Kerala, India. Edappal at the junction of Thrissur-Kuttippuram and Palakkad-Ponnani state highways, is located 45 km (28 mi) from Malappuram.

topPlaces.name: Vattamkulam
topPlaces.description: Vattamkulam is a village in Malappuram district of the Indian state of Kerala. Vattamkulam means "Round Pond".

topPlaces.name: Puramundekkadu Shri Mahadeva Temple
topPlaces.description: Puramundekkadu Shri Mahadeva Temple is an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva is situated at Edappal of Malappuram District in Kerala state in India. The presiding deity of the temple is Shiva, located in main Sanctum Sanctorum, facing East.

topPlaces.name: Chamravattam
topPlaces.description: Chamravattam is a village located in Malappuram district, Kerala, India. This village is on the shores of the Bharathapuzha.

topPlaces.name: Thavanur
topPlaces.description: Thavanur (Tavanur, Thavanoor, or Tavanoor) is a village on the southern bank of the Bharathapuzha, the longest river in Kerala, in the Malappuram District of Kerala State, India. It is located at the border of Tirur and Ponnani Taluks.

topPlaces.name: Vasudevapuram Tavanur
topPlaces.description: Sri Vasudevapuram Temple is one of three ancient temples (Maha-Kshetram) on the Southern bank of holy river Bharathapuzha. It sits east of the other two, Maha-Shiva Temple and Brahma temple.

topPlaces.name: Anakkara (Palakkad)
topPlaces.description: Anakkara is a village and grama panchayat in Pattambi taluk, Palakkad district, Kerala, India, bordering Malappuram district. It is located on the southern bank of Bharathappuzha river (Nila, Ponnani River, or Kuttippuram River).

topPlaces.name: Panthavoor
topPlaces.description: Panthavoor is a small village in Ponnani taluk of Malappuram district in the Indian state of Kerala. This village stands in Alamkode Panchayath.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Perumparampu Sri Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Perumparampu Sri Mahadeva Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Perumparampu Sri Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Calicut International Airport, Kozhikode (37 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (98 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Perumparampu Sri Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Perumparampu Sri Mahadeva Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Perumparampu Sri Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Perumparampu Sri Mahadeva Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Perumparampu Sri Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Edappal, Vattamkulam, Puramundekkadu Shri Mahadeva Temple, Chamravattam, Thavanur.
